text:
gonna use up all my codex too
DUDE
the TOKENS my gosh
10 min and 176k tokens to write a 127 LINE PLAN FILE. that's .... insane
I can see already that token efficiency is gonna be KEY in our factory and I don't know SQUAT about it

ok, having it write tech plans incrementally using high instead of xhigh reasoning. meaning, i'm re-using the same codex session, under the assumption that the context window is already loaded up with useful tokens
moved my Claude Code extra spend limit from $50 to $100
I'm gonna run out of Codex... just writing tech plans is gonna take 2 more hours! didn't see that as the bottleneck
switched to personal [Codex account]. I now understand why the factory folks have multiple claude max plans
I don't even know how to give them more $$
switched to API key. i don't wanna wait til 6am. i'll put a $50 cap on it
plan is ready to go. i'm priming Opus
i put Opus in charge of manual QA before merge, using a browser. i can't believe I'm about to do this
sheesh what a rush
GOOO GOOOO GOOOO
wow paying real $$ for issues is... expensive. I think that first issue was $4? but it's working!!
hoo boy next up is 5 way parallelism? tokens gonna MOVE
woo Claude 5 hr window reset. that's nice
wheels coming off in all kinds of ways with Claude as orchestrator. also the orchestration should be software, not an agent. this is silly. it's a workflow
5k views on my live-tweet thread. dude we have a friggin factory - janky and way too expensive and holy crap it works
first auto-merged PR!! pretty much the first time an AI merged for us
4x parallelism in the factory
holy crap we can't even 4x. api rate limits lol. actually it's really good we're using Claude for this because it's working around all kinds of errors
love how aggressive Opus is being at working through errors and reasoning about what to put through
lol Opus! Codex wasn't getting it done and I told it not to stop under any circumstances so it's like "FINE I'LL DO IT MYSELF"
this seems like it's about to make a huge mess but I'm just gonna ... let it rip. because learning. and I can analyze the session logs in the morning. there are spending caps
and now I officially need a $200 max sub! $100 is not enough.
opus is now jumping around worktrees like a madman

opus is a disaster right now, forgetting steps, going off the rails. i mean, this thing is moving FAST but it's also a friggin rickety train and i'm having to bump it back onto the tracks every 5 min or so
ok, I think i've given enough guidance and it's slowing down. i better go get ~2 hrs sleep or I'm gonna be trashed tmrw

having to remind Opus to do things like clear code review comments, do manual QA. Opus is a capable problem solver, but not great at doing things consistently. So obvious that a software factory needs both! Deterministic flow and a capable LLM on hand to fix stuff
and now... merge hell 5 PRs hitting at once
new instruction: WIP limits. but hey, 3 things merged in!
ok removing parallelism and some of my updates to opus's instructions / runbooks. gives me a little more confidence. still a little scared to go to sleep but gonna try
Danvers checks in (screenshot)
wow it's still going
Upped the parallelism to 2x. Woke up this morning, rolled over in bed, checked computer, and… we did it! Opus you beautiful beast! 11/20 complete tickets. 5+ hour continuous runtime, no steering!!
ugh opus is a TERRIBLE orchestrator. i said bump parallelism to 2 at a time, and it ran 5 at a time
Took a shower. Gonna do an after action review here in just a minute
showered. in the office. 1 ticket left!!!
PR is posted - being reviewed right now. i have a feeling this is gonna fall apart in so many ways but haven't looked yet
[Danvers] Yup so many ways and then it's figuring out time saved or no? But gotta run factory to find defects. This one may be all defects
so much learning though
